title: NOC 93101 – Central control and process operators, petroleum, gas and chemical processing | GTR Immigration
description: As Canada transitions toward "Net Zero" by 2050, the role of petroleum and chemical process operators in 2026 has expanded to include hydrogen production and carbon capture monitoring, making this a future-proof career path.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category9 – Occupations in manufacturing and utilitiesTEER Category2Major Group93 – Central control and process operators

duties: Monitoring and operating petroleum, petrochemical, and chemical plants via centralized control systems.
Adjusting equipment, valves, and pumps to regulate flow and pressure.
Performing statistical process control and analyzing test samples.
Developing emergency shutdown procedures and participating in safety audits.
employment_requirements: Education: Post-secondary diploma in process operation or engineering technology (e.g., Power or Chemical Engineering).
Certification: Power Engineering Certificate (4th to 2nd Class) is mandatory for most positions.
Experience: Several years of experience in the field or a related trade are required.
job_outlook: Employment growth is supported by large-scale energy projects. Automation may limit new job creation, but replacement needs for retiring workers remain critical.
labour_market_demand: Alberta: Very high demand for operators in carbon-neutral oil sands projects and hydrogen hubs.
British Columbia: Significant growth in the Northeast and North Coast regions due to LNG exports.
Saskatchewan: Steady demand in potash and chemical fertilizer processing.
Why Work in Canada as a Petroleum/Chemical Operator?
Market-Leading Salaries: This NOC remains one of the highest-earning non-managerial roles in the country.
Safety-First Culture: Canada leads the world in process safety and environmental protection standards.
Cross-Sector Mobility: Skills are highly transferable between petroleum, pharmaceuticals, and green-energy startups.
Incentivized Settlement: Many roles are in regions with lower costs of living and high PNP success rates.
pathways: Express Entry: High eligibility for the Trade Occupations category draws.
Alberta Opportunity Stream: A direct path for those working in the province's energy sector.
